15.reason: [Using `gets` is unsafe and deprecated; it may cause buffer overflow. More critically, in many modern environments (like those with strict compilation flags or newer C standards), `gets` is not available, leading to a compilation error.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Using `gets` is unsafe and deprecated; it may cause buffer overflow. More critically, in many modern environments (like those with strict compilation flags or newer C standards), `gets` is not available, leading to a compilation error.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 1.0 , reason:  [The function 'gets' is unsafe and has been removed from the C standard library since C11; many modern compilers (like GCC) no longer support it by default, leading to a compilation error. Use 'fgets' instead.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 1.0 , reason:  [Using `gets` is unsafe and deprecated; it was removed in C11 and causes compilation errors with modern compilers. Use `fgets` instead.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 1.0 , reason:  [The function `gets` is unsafe and has been removed from the C standard library since C11. Compilers often disable it by default, leading to a compilation error. Use `fgets` instead.]
